verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo nix in æstate, et pluviæ in messe,  sic indecens est stulto gloria.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּשֶּׁ֤לֶג ׀ בַּקַּ֗יִץ וְכַמָּטָ֥ר בַּקָּצִ֑יר כֵּ֤ן לֹֽא־נָאוֶ֖ה לִכְסִ֣יל כָּבֽוֹד׃
verses.douay_rheims: As snow in summer, and rain in harvest, so glory is not seemly for a fool.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin lumi suvella ja sede elonaikana, niin ei sovi tyhmälle kunnia.
verses.king_james: As snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honour is not seemly for a fool.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut avis ad alia transvolans, et passer quolibet vadens,  sic maledictum frustra prolatum in quempiam superveniet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּצִּפּ֣וֹר לָ֭נוּד כַּדְּר֣וֹר לָע֑וּף כֵּ֥ן קִֽלְלַ֥ת חִ֝נָּ֗ם (לא) [ל֣וֹ] תָבֹֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: As a bird flying to other places, and a sparrow going here or there: so a curse uttered without cause shall come upon a man.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin lintu menee pois, ja pääskynen lentää, niin ei sovi salvaa kirous ilman syytä.
verses.king_james: As the bird by wandering, as the swallow by flying, so the curse causeless shall not come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Flagellum equo, et camus asino,  et virga in dorso imprudentium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שׁ֣וֹט לַ֭סּוּס מֶ֣תֶג לַחֲמ֑וֹר וְ֝שֵׁ֗בֶט לְגֵ֣ו כְּסִילִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: A whip for a horse, and a snaffle for an ass, and a rod for the back of fools.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hevoselle ruoska ja aasille ohjat, ja hullulle vitsa selkään.
verses.king_james: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the ass, and a rod for the fool’s back.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne respondeas stulto juxta stultitiam suam,  ne efficiaris ei similis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תַּ֣עַן כְּ֭סִיל כְּאִוַּלְתּ֑וֹ פֶּֽן־תִּשְׁוֶה־לּ֥וֹ גַם־אָֽתָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest thou be made like him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä vastaa tyhmää hänen tyhmyytensä jälkeen, ettes myös sinä hänen kaltaiseksensa tulisi.
verses.king_james: Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest thou also be like unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responde stulto juxta stultitiam suam,  ne sibi sapiens esse videatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲנֵ֣ה כְ֭סִיל כְּאִוַּלְתּ֑וֹ פֶּן־יִהְיֶ֖ה חָכָ֣ם בְּעֵינָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he imagine himself to be wise.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vastaa tyhmää hänen tyhmyytensä jälkeen, ettei hän näkyisi viisaaksi silmissänsä.
verses.king_james: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own conceit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Claudus pedibus, et iniquitatem bibens,  qui mittit verba per nuntium stultum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְקַצֶּ֣ה רַ֭גְלַיִם חָמָ֣ס שֹׁתֶ֑ה שֹׁלֵ֖חַ דְּבָרִ֣ים בְּיַד־כְּסִֽיל׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that sendeth words by a foolish messenger, is lame of feet and drinketh iniquity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka tyhmäin sanansaattajain kautta asian toimittaa, hän on niinkuin rampa jaloista, ja saa vahingon.
verses.king_james: He that sendeth a message by the hand of a fool cutteth off the feet, and drinketh damage.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo pulchras frustra habet claudus tibias,  sic indecens est in ore stultorum parabola.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דַּלְי֣וּ שֹׁ֭קַיִם מִפִּסֵּ֑חַ וּ֝מָשָׁ֗ל בְּפִ֣י כְסִילִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: As a lame man hath fair legs in vain: so a parable is unseemly in the mouth of fools.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin nilkku voi hypätä, niin tyhmä taitaa puhua viisaudesta.
verses.king_james: The legs of the lame are not equal: so is a parable in the mouth of fools.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut qui mittit lapidem in acervum Mercurii,  ita qui tribuit insipienti honorem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּצְר֣וֹר אֶ֭בֶן בְּמַרְגֵּמָ֑ה כֵּן־נוֹתֵ֖ן לִכְסִ֣יל כָּבֽוֹד׃
verses.douay_rheims: As he that casteth a stone into the heap of Mercury: so is he that giveth honour to a fool.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka tyhmälle taritsee kunniaa, on niinkuin joku heittäisi kalliin kiven kiviroukkioon.
verses.king_james: As he that bindeth a stone in a sling, so is he that giveth honour to a fool.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo si spina nascatur in manu temulenti,  sic parabola in ore stultorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ח֭וֹחַ עָלָ֣ה בְיַד־שִׁכּ֑וֹר וּ֝מָשָׁ֗ל בְּפִ֣י כְסִילִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: As if a thorn should grow in the hand of a drunkard: so is a parable in the mouth of fools.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sananlasku on tyhmäin suussa niinkuin orjantappura, joka juopuneen käsiin pistelee.
verses.king_james: As a thorn goeth up into the hand of a drunkard, so is a parable in the mouth of fools.

verses.latin_vulgate: Judicium determinat causas,  et qui imponit stulto silentium iras mitigat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רַ֥ב מְחוֹלֵֽל־כֹּ֑ל וְשֹׂכֵ֥ר כְּ֝סִ֗יל וְשֹׂכֵ֥ר עֹבְרִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Judgment determineth causes: and he that putteth a fool to silence, appeaseth anger.
verses.finnish_biblia: Taitava ihminen tekee kappaleen oikein; vaan joka taitamattoman palkkaa, hän sen turmelee.
verses.king_james: The great God that formed all things both rewardeth the fool, and rewardeth transgressors.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut canis qui revertitur ad vomitum suum,  sic imprudens qui iterat stultitiam suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּ֭כֶלֶב שָׁ֣ב עַל־קֵא֑וֹ כְּ֝סִ֗יל שׁוֹנֶ֥ה בְאִוַּלְתּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: As a dog that returneth to his vomit, so is the fool that repeateth his folly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin koira syö oksennuksensa, niin on hullu joka hulluutensa kertoo.
verses.king_james: As a dog returneth to his vomit, so a fool returneth to his folly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidisti hominem sapientem sibi videri ?  magis illo spem habebit insipiens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָאִ֗יתָ אִ֭ישׁ חָכָ֣ם בְּעֵינָ֑יו תִּקְוָ֖ה לִכְסִ֣יל מִמֶּֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hast thou seen a man wise in his own conceit? there shall be more hope of a fool than of him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koskas näet jonkun, joka luulee itsensä viisaaksi, enempi on silloin toivoa tyhmästä kuin hänestä.
verses.king_james: Seest thou a man wise in his own conceit? there is more hope of a fool than of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit piger : Leo est in via,  et leæna in itineribus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָמַ֣ר עָ֭צֵל שַׁ֣חַל בַּדָּ֑רֶךְ אֲ֝רִ֗י בֵּ֣ין הָרְחֹבֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: The slothful man saith: There is a lion in the way, and a lioness in the roads.
verses.finnish_biblia: Laiska sanoo: nuori jalopeura on tiellä, ja jalopeura kujalla.
verses.king_james: The slothful man saith, There is a lion in the way; a lion is in the streets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut ostium vertitur in cardine suo,  ita piger in lectulo suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַ֭דֶּלֶת תִּסּ֣וֹב עַל־צִירָ֑הּ וְ֝עָצֵ֗ל עַל־מִטָּתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: As the door turneth upon its hinges, so doth the slothful upon his bed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Laiska kääntelee itsiänsä vuoteella niinkuin ovi saranassa.
verses.king_james: As the door turneth upon his hinges, so doth the slothful upon his bed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abscondit piger manum sub ascella sua,  et laborat si ad os suum eam converterit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טָ֘מַ֤ן עָצֵ֣ל יָ֭דוֹ בַּצַּלָּ֑חַת נִ֝לְאָ֗ה לַהֲשִׁיבָ֥הּ אֶל־פִּֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: The slothful hideth his hand under his armpit, and it grieveth him to turn it to his mouth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Laiska panee kätensä poveensa: ja se tulee hänelle työlääksi saattaa suuhunsa jälleen.
verses.king_james: The slothful hideth his hand in his bosom; it grieveth him to bring it again to his mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sapientior sibi piger videtur  septem viris loquentibus sententias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָכָ֣ם עָצֵ֣ל בְּעֵינָ֑יו מִ֝שִּׁבְעָ֗ה מְשִׁ֣יבֵי טָֽעַם׃
verses.douay_rheims: The sluggard is wiser in his own conceit, than seven men that speak sentences.
verses.finnish_biblia: Laiska luulee itsensä viisaammaksi kuin seitsemän, jotka hyviä tapoja opettavat.
verses.king_james: The sluggard is wiser in his own conceit than seven men that can render a reason.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut qui apprehendit auribus canem,  sic qui transit impatiens et commiscetur rixæ alterius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַחֲזִ֥יק בְּאׇזְנֵי־כָ֑לֶב עֹבֵ֥ר מִ֝תְעַבֵּ֗ר עַל־רִ֥יב לֹּא־לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: As he that taketh a dog by the ears, so is he that passeth by in anger, and meddleth with another man's quarrel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka käy ohitse ja sekoittaa itsensä muiden riitoihin, hän on senkaltainen, joka koiraa korvista vetää.
verses.king_james: He that passeth by, and meddleth with strife belonging not to him, is like one that taketh a dog by the ears.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut noxius est qui mittit sagittas et lanceas in mortem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּֽ֭מִתְלַהְלֵהַּ הַיֹּרֶ֥ה זִקִּ֗ים חִצִּ֥ים וָמָֽוֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: As he is guilty that shooteth arrows, and lances unto death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin se, joka salaa ampuu, tarkoittaa joutsella ja tappaa,
verses.king_james: As a mad man who casteth firebrands, arrows, and death,

verses.latin_vulgate: ita vir fraudulenter nocet amico suo,  et cum fuerit deprehensus dicit : Ludens feci.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֵּֽן־אִ֭ישׁ רִמָּ֣ה אֶת־רֵעֵ֑הוּ וְ֝אָמַ֗ר הֲֽלֹא־מְשַׂחֵ֥ק אָֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: So is the man that hurteth his friend deceitfully: and when he is taken, saith: I did it in jest.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin on petollinen ihminen hänen lähimmäisillensä, ja sanoo: leikillä minä sen tein.
verses.king_james: So is the man that deceiveth his neighbour, and saith, Am not I in sport?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum defecerint ligna extinguetur ignis,  et susurrone subtracto, jurgia conquiescent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאֶ֣פֶס עֵ֭צִים תִּכְבֶּה־אֵ֑שׁ וּבְאֵ֥ין נִ֝רְגָּ֗ן יִשְׁתֹּ֥ק מָדֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: When the wood faileth, the fire shall go out: and when the talebearer is taken away, contentions shall cease.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin halot loppuvat, niin tuli sammuu, ja kuin kielikellot pannaan pois, niin riidat lakkaavat.
verses.king_james: Where no wood is, there the fire goeth out: so where there is no talebearer, the strife ceaseth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut carbones ad prunas, et ligna ad ignem,  sic homo iracundus suscitat rixas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פֶּחָ֣ם לְ֭גֶחָלִים וְעֵצִ֣ים לְאֵ֑שׁ וְאִ֥ישׁ (מדונים) [מִ֝דְיָנִ֗ים] לְחַרְחַר־רִֽיב׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: As coals are to burning coals, and wood to fire, so an angry man stirreth up strife.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin hiilet tuleen, ja puut liekkiin, niin riitainen ihminen saattaa metelin.
verses.king_james: As coals are to burning coals, and wood to fire; so is a contentious man to kindle strife.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verba susurronis quasi simplicia,  et ipsa perveniunt ad intima ventris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דִּבְרֵ֣י נִ֭רְגָּן כְּמִֽתְלַהֲמִ֑ים וְ֝הֵ֗ם יָרְד֥וּ חַדְרֵי־בָֽטֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: The words of a talebearer are as it were simple, but they reach to the innermost parts of the belly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Panettelian sanat ovat niinkuin haavat, ja käyvät läpi sydämen.
verses.king_james: The words of a talebearer are as wounds, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo si argento sordido ornare velis vas fictile,  sic labia tumentia cum pessimo corde sociata.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֶּ֣סֶף סִ֭יגִים מְצֻפֶּ֣ה עַל־חָ֑רֶשׂ שְׂפָתַ֖יִם דֹּלְקִ֣ים וְלֶב־רָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: Swelling lips joined with a corrupt heart, are like an earthern vessel adorned with silver dross.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viekas kieli ja paha sydän on niinkuin saviastia hopian savulla juotettu.
verses.king_james: Burning lips and a wicked heart are like a potsherd covered with silver dross.

verses.latin_vulgate: Labiis suis intelligitur inimicus,  cum in corde tractaverit dolos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּ֭שְׂפָתָו יִנָּכֵ֣ר שׂוֹנֵ֑א וּ֝בְקִרְבּ֗וֹ יָשִׁ֥ית מִרְמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: An enemy is known by his lips, when in his heart he entertaineth deceit.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vihamien teeskelee itsensä toisin puheellansa; mutta petos on hänen sydämessänsä.
verses.king_james: He that hateth dissembleth with his lips, and layeth up deceit within him;

verses.latin_vulgate: Quando submiserit vocem suam, ne credideris ei,  quoniam septem nequitiæ sunt in corde illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־יְחַנֵּ֣ן ק֭וֹלוֹ אַל־תַּאֲמֶן־בּ֑וֹ כִּ֤י שֶׁ֖בַע תּוֹעֵב֣וֹת בְּלִבּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: When he shall speak low, trust him not: because there are seven mischiefs in his heart.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin hän liehakoitsee makeilla puheillansa, niin älä usko häntä; sillä seitsemän kauhistusta on hänen sydämessänsä.
verses.king_james: When he speaketh fair, believe him not: for there are seven abominations in his heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui operit odium fraudulenter,  revelabitur malitia ejus in consilio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּכַּסֶּ֣ה שִׂ֭נְאָה בְּמַשָּׁא֑וֹן תִּגָּלֶ֖ה רָעָת֣וֹ בְקָהָֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that covereth hatred deceitfully, his malice shall be laid open in the public assembly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka pitää salavihaa, tehdäksensä vahinkoa, hänen pahuutensa tulee ilmi kokouksissa.
verses.king_james: Whose hatred is covered by deceit, his wickedness shall be shewed before the whole congregation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui fodit foveam incidet in eam,  et qui volvit lapidem revertetur ad eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּֽרֶה־שַּׁ֭חַת בָּ֣הּ יִפּ֑וֹל וְג֥וֹלֵֽל אֶ֝֗בֶן אֵלָ֥יו תָּשֽׁוּב׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that diggeth a pit, shall fall into it: and he that rolleth a stone, it shall return to him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka kuopan kaivaa, hän lankee siihen, ja joka kiven vierittää, se kierittää sen itse päällensä.
verses.king_james: Whoso diggeth a pit shall fall therein: and he that rolleth a stone, it will return upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lingua fallax non amat veritatem,  et os lubricum operatur ruinas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְֽשׁוֹן־שֶׁ֭קֶר יִשְׂנָ֣א דַכָּ֑יו וּפֶ֥ה חָ֝לָ֗ק יַעֲשֶׂ֥ה מִדְחֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: A deceitful tongue loveth not truth: and a slippery mouth worketh ruin.
verses.finnish_biblia: Petollinen kieli vihaa rangaistusta, ja suu, joka myötäkielin puhuu, saattaa kadotukseen.
verses.king_james: A lying tongue hateth those that are afflicted by it; and a flattering mouth worketh ruin.
